title: OMR response import: upload a candidate-responses CSV into an import batch (the chain's real entry point)
business_reason: SPEC_VS_BUILT_GAP evaluation_ops: 'OMR/CSV import not wired (importConfig absent)'. Scoring (FR-0008) consumed seeded evaluation_candidate_responses; nothing imported them. This wires the upstream: parse an uploaded responses CSV and persist evaluation_candidate_responses, so the whole exam chain (import → score → handoff → rank → cert) starts from real input. Reuses the roster CSV tokenizer (FR-0002).

design.parser: server/eval/omrIngest.ts parseOmrResponses(csv) PURE (reuses parseCsv): header must have candidate_id + Q1.. columns; each row -> {candidate_id, responses:{Q1:..}}; flags blank + duplicate candidate rows; ignores non-Q columns. Unit-tested.
design.service: ingestOmrBatch({actor, importBatchId, content}): load import_batch -> require school_id; parse; UPSERT evaluation_candidate_responses (import_batch_id, candidate_id, school_id=batch.school_id, response_payload, status 'parsed') on the 0023 natural key; set import_batch counts + batch_status='validated'; audited.

param_14.question: How do candidate OMR responses get into the system?
param_14.answer: A staff user uploads a responses CSV (candidate_id + Q1.. columns) to a school-scoped import batch; the server parses it (pure parser), attributes the school from the batch, and persists evaluation_candidate_responses (one per candidate, idempotent). Scoring (FR-0008) then runs against the approved answer key.
param_14.security_implications: responses are restricted staff data; school attributed server-side from the import batch (not client per-row); idempotent upsert (re-import replaces, no dup rows); blank/duplicate rows reported not silently dropped; audited. Binary raw-scan storage (source_file uuid) deferred — the parsed rows are the artifact.
param_14.approval_implications: none; downstream scoring requires an approved answer key (FR-0008).
param_14.downstream_dependencies: evaluation_candidate_responses feed scoreBatch (FR-0008) -> scores -> handoff (FR-0009) -> ranking -> certificate. Per-candidate raw-scan image + parser_confidence (real OMR scan parsing) deferred.
param_14.assumptions: one school per import batch (import_batch.school_id); CSV columns are candidate_id + Q1.., values are option letters.
